hyperedges.label: Post-Login Internal Dashboard UI Capture
hyperedges.description: The screenshot captures the InternalLayout using ApplicationShell to render the desktop dashboard view — together they represent the post-login entry point for internal (admin) users of the LICI platform after the UX migration.

hyperedges.label: UX Migration Visual Evidence Chain
hyperedges.description: The screenshot serves as visual evidence for the UX migration validation, which confirms the unified visual system (tokens + base CSS) is correctly applied across the application post-migration.

hyperedges.label: before/after UX evidence group
hyperedges.description: This screenshot is part of the before/after visual evidence trio used in ux-validation.md to document the migration defined in ux-migration-plan.md.

nodes.label: Graphify Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent edges, never skip corpus check warning, always show token cost, never hide cohesion scores behind symbols, never run HTML viz on >5000 nodes without warning.

nodes.label: Gemini LLM Backend for Semantic Extraction
nodes.norm_label: gemini llm backend for semantic extraction

nodes.label: Parallel Subagent Dispatch for Semantic Extraction
nodes.norm_label: parallel subagent dispatch for semantic extraction

nodes.label: Graphify Exports System (Neo4j, FalkorDB, Wiki, MCP)
nodes.norm_label: graphify exports system (neo4j, falkordb, wiki, mcp)

nodes.label: No Fixed LAN IP Design
nodes.rationale: No fixed IP in code; services use localhost internally, frontend uses relative /api/v1, external users connect via current server IP.

nodes.label: Route and Screen Inventory (public, supplier, internal)
nodes.norm_label: route and screen inventory (public, supplier, internal)

nodes.label: Single Icon Library Constraint (Lucide React)
nodes.rationale: Conserve a single icon library (Lucide React) as the only source of icons to maintain consistency and avoid bundle bloat.

nodes.label: Design Token CSS Files (tokens.css, base.css, components.css)
nodes.norm_label: design token css files (tokens.css, base.css, components.css)

nodes.label: P1: Missing Rate Limiting and Account Locking
nodes.norm_label: p1: missing rate limiting and account locking

nodes.label: P1: No Refresh Token Rotation or Revocation
nodes.norm_label: p1: no refresh token rotation or revocation

nodes.label: P1: Password Reset Flow is a Stub
nodes.norm_label: p1: password reset flow is a stub

nodes.label: P1: Concurrent Tender Code Generation Race Condition
nodes.norm_label: p1: concurrent tender code generation race condition

nodes.label: P1: File Upload Accepts Client-Supplied MIME Type
nodes.norm_label: p1: file upload accepts client-supplied mime type

nodes.label: P2: ESLint Not Configured in Backend
nodes.norm_label: p2: eslint not configured in backend

nodes.label: P2: Audit Logging Uses Void Pattern (fire-and-forget)
nodes.norm_label: p2: audit logging uses void pattern (fire-and-forget)

nodes.label: P2: Document Status Uses Inline Object Without DTO
nodes.norm_label: p2: document status uses inline object without dto

nodes.label: after-login-desktop.png
nodes.description: Desktop screenshot (1440x1000) of the LICI platform after login, showing the internal dashboard ApplicationShell with sidebar navigation, LICI branding, and main content area. Part of UX validation evidence demonstrating the unified visual system post-migration.

nodes.label: UX Migration Validation
nodes.description: Validation suite executed 2026-07-14 covering 43 routes, shared shell, navigation, responsive drawer, tokens, components, pagination, skeletons, errors, notifications, focus, lazy loading (bundle from ~517 kB to 267 kB).

nodes.label: Design Tokens CSS
nodes.description: CSS custom properties defining the unified visual system tokens shared across public routes, supplier portal, and internal admin.

nodes.label: Base CSS
nodes.description: CSS fundamentals including accessibility rules, skip-link, focus-visible states, and responsive breakpoints.

nodes.label: Desktop Dashboard View
nodes.description: 1440x1000 desktop layout pattern showing sidebar + content shell with LICI green branding, grouped navigation, user avatar with initials, and the Resumen (dashboard) page as default post-login view.

nodes.label: before-admin-suppliers.png
nodes.description: Pre-migration screenshot of the supplier management page for comparison with the post-migration UI.

nodes.label: after-login-mobile.png
nodes.description: Mobile UI screenshot showing the application state immediately after user login

nodes.label: After Login Screen (Mobile)
nodes.description: Post-authentication landing screen UI rendered on mobile viewport

nodes.label: Mobile Layout
nodes.description: Responsive mobile layout adaptation of the after-login interface, likely with stacked/hamburger navigation and touch-optimized elements

nodes.label: Authenticated Application State
nodes.description: The application state after successful authentication, representing authorized user access to the licitaciones platform

nodes.label: before-admin-suppliers.png
nodes.description: Screenshot of the admin suppliers management page in its original (pre-migration) visual state. 1353x533 PNG desktop capture showing the legacy interface at /internal/suppliers before the UX token system and AppShell redesign.

nodes.label: SuppliersManagementPage
nodes.description: Admin page listing suppliers with columns for RUC, legal name, status badge, and a detail link. Uses PageHeader with 'Gestión de proveedores' title and 'Añadir proveedor' action link. Data fetched via TanStack Query from /suppliers endpoint.

nodes.label: ux-validation.md
nodes.description: Final UX validation report documenting the migration's completion status, coverage of 43 routes, token system, responsive shell, and before/after visual evidence.

nodes.label: ux-audit.md
nodes.description: UX audit identifying issues in the original interface: inline visual values, lack of token system, missing skeletons/empty states/toasts, 700+ line global CSS, two monolithic page files, inconsistent controls, and accessibility gaps.

nodes.label: ux-migration-plan.md
nodes.description: 5-phase migration plan to establish design tokens, shared AppShell, migrate all screens, add responsive support, and validate. Defines commit strategy and risk controls.

nodes.label: ux-screenshots/
nodes.description: Directory containing PNG screenshots for UX validation: one before-state capture of the admin suppliers page and two after-state captures (desktop and mobile login).

nodes.label: favicon.svg
nodes.description: SVG favicon for the licitaciones application; 64x64 rounded square icon with dark teal (#0c594d) background and a white stylized document/paper shape with text lines, representing a tender document or form.

nodes.label: Document Motif
nodes.description: White SVG path shape depicting a document or paper with a tab/fold and horizontal text lines, rendered inside the favicon.

nodes.label: Teal Brand Color
nodes.description: Dark teal fill color #0c594d used for the favicon's rounded-rectangle background; a color commonly associated with government and institutional interfaces in Spanish-speaking public-sector software.

links.explanation: The screenshot shows the InternalLayout wrapping ApplicationShell with internal navigation items.
InternalLayout.tsx line 11 imports and renders ApplicationShell component.
The screenshot captures the ApplicationShell rendered in the internal dashboard layout.

Both screenshots are part of the UX migration evidence, with before-admin-suppliers showing pre-migration state and after-login-desktop showing post-migration state. They form a before/after comparison pattern.
Cited in docs/ux-validation.md line 44 as evidencia visual 'Después: acceso desktop'.
The screenshot indirectly demonstrates the visual system tokens (colors, spacing, typography) referenced in ux-validation.md as part of the unified visual language.
The screenshot file directly captures the desktop dashboard view of the LICI platform.
The desktop dashboard view is one of the key visual artifacts validated during the UX migration, as the Resumen (dashboard) page is the default post-login view.
The desktop view is styled using the unified design tokens system referenced in ux-validation.md line 63.
The desktop view incorporates base CSS accessibility rules including skip-link and focus states.

links.label: visualizes_findings_of
links.description: The screenshot demonstrates the UX issues documented in the audit: the legacy CSS with inline values, the old PageHeader/DataTable without token-based styles, and the absence of AppShell.

links.label: pre_migration_state_for
links.description: The screenshot preserves the pre-migration visual state that the migration plan phases 1-4 aimed to systematically replace with token-based, responsive UI.

links.label: contained_in
links.description: The PNG file resides in the ux-screenshots directory.

links.label: evidence_in
links.description: ux-validation.md references this screenshot as the 'before' evidence at line 43: '[Antes: gestión de proveedores](ux-screenshots/before-admin-suppliers.png)'.

links.label: depicts
links.description: The screenshot captures the visual output of the SuppliersManagementPage React component at route /internal/suppliers.

links.label: audited_in
links.description: The SuppliersManagementPage component at /internal/suppliers is one of the screens covered by the UX audit, falling under monolithic InternalPages.tsx and inline-styles concerns.

links.label: validates_completion_of
links.description: ux-validation.md confirms that all phases of the migration plan were completed successfully, with before/after screenshots as evidence.

links.label: informs
links.description: The audit's findings of visual inconsistency, lack of token system, and missing components directly informed the migration plan's phased approach.
The SVG path elements (d='M18 17h8v24h20v7H18V17Zm13 0h8v19h-8V17Z') explicitly draw a white document/paper shape with a tab and text lines.
The SVG rect element explicitly specifies fill='#0c594d' for the 64x64 rounded background.
The white (#fff) document motif is rendered on top of the dark teal (#0c594d) background, creating a high-contrast icon design.
